title: Why Your Future Self Feels Like a Stranger
subtitle: 如何更好地实现长期目标

paragraphs.en: Hal Hershfield, a psychologist at the UCLA Anderson School of Management, wanted to know why people weren't saving for retirement. Across the board, people are living longer. Logically, they'll need more money to live comfortably in their post-work years. And yet, savings rates in the U.S. have gone down in recent decades, not up.
paragraphs.zh: 加州大学洛杉矶分校安德森管理学院的心理学家哈尔·赫什菲尔德想知道人们为何不为退休储蓄。总体来看，人们的寿命越来越长。按逻辑，他们需要更多钱才能在退休后舒适生活。然而近几十年来，美国的储蓄率不升反降。

paragraphs.en: To help explain this seemingly irrational behavior, Hershfield and his team scanned the brains of study participants while asking them to what degree various traits — like 'honorable' or 'funny' — applied to their current self, their future self, a current other, or a future other. As participants answered, Hershfield's team recorded which parts of their brains lit up. Unsurprisingly, people's brains were most active when thinking about their current selves and least active when thinking about a current other.
paragraphs.zh: 为解释这种看似不理性的行为，赫什菲尔德及其团队扫描了研究参与者的大脑，同时询问他们“可敬”或“有趣”等各种特质在多大程度上适用于当前的自己、未来的自己、当前的他人或未来的他人。当参与者作答时，赫什菲尔德的团队记录下他们大脑中活跃的区域。不出所料，人们在思考当前的自己时大脑最活跃，思考当前的他人时最不活跃。

words.meaning: adj. 不理性的，不合逻辑的

paragraphs.en: But the team found that participants' brain activity while considering their future selves more closely resembled their brain activity while thinking about a current other rather than the current self. Put in practical terms, when thinking of yourself in a month or a year or a decade, your brain registers that person in ways similar to how it would register Taylor Swift or the mailman. Understood in that way, saving for retirement is the equivalent of giving money away to someone else entirely.
paragraphs.zh: 但研究小组发现，参与者在考虑未来自己时的大脑活动，更接近于思考当前他人而非当前自己时的大脑活动。用通俗的话说，当你想到一个月、一年或十年后的自己时，大脑对那个人的识别方式类似于识别泰勒·斯威夫特或邮递员。如此理解，为退休储蓄就等同于把钱完全送给别人。

paragraphs.en: In light of Hershfield's study, one simple question arises: Is it possible to make our present selves give a damn about our future selves? The answers are anything but simple. Seen through the lens of the present self versus the future self, our self-defeating actions — like choosing to watch television rather than go to the gym — suddenly make perfect sense. We get to enjoy the very concrete, immediate benefits of our actions while someone else (namely, our future selves) suffers the hypothetical, far-off consequences.
paragraphs.zh: 根据赫什菲尔德的研究，一个简单的问题浮现出来：我们能否让现在的自己在意未来的自己？答案绝不简单。从当前自我与未来自我对比的视角来看，我们的自我挫败行为——比如选择看电视而不去健身房——突然变得完全合理。我们得以享受行动带来的具体、即时好处，而别人（也就是未来的自己）则承受假想的、遥远的后果。

words.meaning: adj. 自我挫败的，适得其反的

paragraphs.en: As a result, the decisions we make for our present selves often look very different from our decisions for our future selves. We believe that tomorrow will be different. We believe that we will be different tomorrow; but in doing so, we prioritize our current mood over the consequences of our inaction for the future self. Understanding our procrastination through the lens of the present and future selves, we're left with three possible solutions.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，我们为当前自己做的决定往往与为未来自己做的决定大相径庭。我们相信明天会不同。我们相信明天的自己会不同；但如此一来，我们优先考虑当前情绪，而忽视不作为对未来自己造成的后果。从当前与未来自我的视角理解拖延症，我们得出三种可能的解决方案。

paragraphs.en: The first is to force your future self to do whatever your present self doesn't want to do. The second is to convince your present self that your future self is, in fact, still you. If the central problem is that we think of our future selves as other people, it follows that trying to identify more closely with our future selves will encourage us to make better long-term decisions. In a follow-up study, Hershfield wanted to explore ways to bridge the disconnect between the present and future selves and encourage people to save more for retirement.
paragraphs.zh: 第一种是强迫未来自己去做当前自己不愿做的事。第二种是说服当前自己，未来自己实际上仍是你。如果核心问题在于我们将未来自己视为他人，那么尝试更紧密地认同未来自己将鼓励我们做出更好的长期决策。在一项后续研究中，赫什菲尔德想探索如何弥合当前与未来自我的脱节，鼓励人们为退休储蓄更多。

paragraphs.en: He and his team took photos of study participants, and then used image processing to visually age their faces. Participants were then placed in a virtual reality setting where they could look into a mirror and see their aged selves looking back at them. Participants who saw their aged selves said they would save 30% more of their salary for retirement than the control group. Whatever your long-term goals may be — getting in better shape, launching your own business, writing a book — thinking about your deadline in terms of days rather than months or years can help you wrap your mind around how close the future really is.
paragraphs.zh: 他和团队为研究参与者拍照，然后用图像处理技术将他们的面容视觉老化。随后参与者被置于虚拟现实环境中，可以照镜子看到年老的自己回望他们。看到年老自己的参与者表示，他们将为退休储蓄比对照组多30%的工资。无论你的长期目标是什么——改善体型、创业、写书——以天而非月或年来思考截止日期，有助于你真切感受到未来有多近。

paragraphs.en: The third solution is to forget about your future self and use your present self's love of instant gratification to your advantage. While the two tactics above can be effective in making better long-term choices, in the end, you're still struggling against human nature. Our brains are hard-wired for instant gratification. Instead of fighting your present self's need for immediate rewards, why not use it to your advantage? When most of us set goals, we focus on long-term results we want to see — e.g., losing weight, getting a promotion, retiring in comfort, etc.
paragraphs.zh: 第三种方案是忘掉未来自己，利用当前自己对即时满足的热爱为自身服务。虽然上述两种策略能有效做出更好的长期选择，但归根结底你仍在与人性作斗争。我们的大脑天生追求即时满足。与其与当前自己对即时奖励的需求对抗，何不加以利用？大多数人设定目标时，关注的是想看到的长期结果——例如减肥、升职、舒适退休等。

paragraphs.en: While those visions of our future selves can be inspiring, when it comes to actually doing the day-to-day work, it may be more effective to reframe activities in terms of their immediate, or at least very near-term, rewards. Take writing this article, for instance. It's easy for me to imagine how amazing it will feel at the end of the workday to have this article done. This isn't just my opinion. Research partners Kaitlin Woolley of Cornell University and Ayelet Fishbach of the University of Chicago have made a career out of studying the differences between the goals that people achieve and the ones that fall by the wayside.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然对未来自己的那些想象可能鼓舞人心，但在实际进行日常工作时，以即时或至少近期的奖励来重新框定活动或许更有效。以写这篇文章为例。我很容易想象，在工作日结束时完成这篇文章的感觉会有多棒。这不仅是我个人的看法。康奈尔大学的凯特琳·伍利和芝加哥大学的阿耶莱特·菲什巴赫这对研究伙伴致力于研究人们实现的目标与半途而废的目标之间的差异。

words.meaning: v. 重新框定，重新表述

paragraphs.en: In one study, we asked people online about the goals they set at the beginning of the year. Most people set goals to achieve delayed, long-term benefits, such as career advancement, debt repayment, or improved health. We asked these individuals how enjoyable it was to pursue their goal, as well as how important their goal was. We also asked whether they were still working on their goals two months after setting them. We found that enjoyment predicted people's goal persistence two months after setting the goal far more than how important they rated their goal to be, Woolley said.
paragraphs.zh: 伍利说：“在一项研究中，我们在网上询问人们年初设定的目标。大多数人设定的目标是追求延迟的长期利益，如职业发展、偿还债务或改善健康。我们询问这些人追求目标的乐趣有多大，以及目标有多重要。我们还询问他们在设定目标两个月后是否仍在为之努力。我们发现，乐趣比他们自评的目标重要程度更能预测两个月后的目标坚持度。”

paragraphs.en: This pattern held true across a wide variety of goals from exercising to studying to eating healthier foods. For example, people ate 50% more of a healthy food when directed to focus on the good taste rather than the long-term health benefits. Other studies have shown a greater uptake of exercise in people who were told to think of the enjoyment of doing the exercise now rather than future health gains. These findings suggest that when it comes to achieving your goals, enjoying the process itself is more important than wanting the long-term benefits.
paragraphs.zh: 这一模式适用于从锻炼到学习再到健康饮食的各类目标。例如，当人们被引导关注健康食品的美味而非长期健康益处时，他们会多吃50%。其他研究表明，被告知去想当前锻炼的乐趣而非未来健康收益的人，锻炼参与度更高。这些发现表明，在实现目标时，享受过程本身比渴望长期利益更重要。

paragraphs.en: In other words, present self trumps future self. Who says instant gratification has to be a bad thing? By all means, set ambitious long-term goals for your future self, but when it comes to actually following through day-to-day, make sure your present self knows what's in it for her too.
paragraphs.zh: 换言之，当前自我胜过未来自我。谁说即时满足一定是坏事？当然要为未来的自己设定宏大的长期目标，但在日常的实际执行中，务必让当前的自己也知道她能得到什么好处。
